SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.risk.model

Class DualDecoderModel

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.jnn.Module
edu.bu.jnn.Model
edu.bu.pas.risk.model.DualDecoderModel
public class DualDecoderModel extends edu.bu.jnn.Model

This class represents a dual-pipeline neural network with a shared encoder (e.g. two separate decoders and one shared encoder). In our implementation of risk there are two decisions that agent's must make: which territory should I place an army on (when its my turn or during setup), and what action should I pick (when its my turn)?

Therefore to model this using q-value producing neural networks (e.g. q-networks) we should have one network per decision point. However, both decisions require examining the state of the game, so we share an encoder so that learning on placement decisions helps with actions (and vice versa). The point of this shared encoder is to produce a neural network that has learned as "good" a representation of the game state as possible.

game states are converted into feature vectors which are passed through the encoder. The output vector of this encoder contains the "meaning" of the state. Depending on which decoder is required, the corresponding action type is also converted into a feature vector. This feature vector is then concatenated with the state encoding and passed through the appropriate decoder to produce a single q-value Q(s,a) for the action decoder and Q(s,t) for the placement decoder (e.g. choosing a territory to place an army at).

Author:
Andrew Wood

    • actionForward

      public final edu.bu.jmat.Matrix actionForward(edu.bu.jmat.Matrix stateFeatures, edu.bu.jmat.Matrix actionFeatures) throws Exception
      A method to get Q(s,a) by passing the state features through the encoder, concatenating the output vector with the action features and passing that vector through the action decoder.
      Parameters:
      stateFeatures - the feature vector/matrix for the state
      actionFeatures - the feature vector/matrix for the action
      Returns:
      a matrix of q-values. This will either by a column vector or a singleton depending on how many samples are provided in the features.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • actionBackwards

      public final edu.bu.jmat.Pair<edu.bu.jmat.Matrix,edu.bu.jmat.Matrix> actionBackwards(edu.bu.jmat.Matrix stateFeatures, edu.bu.jmat.Matrix actionFeatures, edu.bu.jmat.Matrix dLoss_dActions) throws Exception

      Parameters:
      stateFeatures - the feature vector/matrix for the state
      placementFeatures - the feature vector/matrix for the territory
      Returns:
      a matrix of q-values. This will either by a column vector or a singleton depending on how many samples are provided in the features.
      Throws:
      Exception
